intro: Heliophytum tiaridioides, also known as the 'Tiaridium', is a flowering plant species endemic to Australia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Heliophytum tiaridioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve symptoms of fever and rheumatism.
